observations.title: sdd-init/auth
observations.content:
## Project Context

**Stack**: NestJS 11 + TypeScript 5.7 + TypeORM 0.3 + PostgreSQL + pnpm
**Package manager**: pnpm
**Node target**: ES2023 / CommonJS

## Architecture
Hexagonal / Clean Architecture with 3 layers under `src/auth/`:
- `domain/` → domain models (UserAccount, Role, Permission) + repository interfaces
- `application/` → use cases (signUp, getUserByCredentials, createRole, createPermission, getAllRoles, getAllPermissions)
- `infrastructure/` → controllers (v1/), repositories (Pg* + Memory*), entities (TypeORM), guards, decorators, services

## Current Structure Issue
Everything lives under a single `src/auth/` module. Goal of upcoming refactor: split into domain-oriented modules (e.g., separate domains for user management, roles, permissions) for better scalability.

## Key Files
- `src/auth/auth.module.ts` — main NestJS module
- `src/auth/domain/` — 7 domain files (models + interfaces)
- `src/auth/application/` — 6 use cases
- `src/auth/infrastructure/` — controllers, repos, entities, guards, decorators
- `src/db/` — migrations + seeds (TypeORM)
- `src/app.module.ts` — root module
- `jest.config.ts` — Jest config (rootDir: src, testRegex: *.spec.ts)
- `test/jest-e2e.json` — E2E jest config (testRegex: .e2e-spec.ts)

## Quality Tools
- ESLint 9 (eslint.config.mjs)
- Prettier
- TypeScript strict (strictNullChecks, noImplicitAny: false)

## Conventions
- DTOs via class-validator
- Memory repos for unit/integration tests
- @nestjs/testing TestingModule for controller specs
- conventional commits

observations.title: sdd/auth/domain-refactor/explore
observations.content:
## Exploración: domain-refactor

**Fecha**: 2026-04-02

### Estado actual

El módulo `auth` contiene tres dominios cohesivos (permissions, roles, users) más lógica de sesión JWT, todos acoplados en un único módulo NestJS.

**Domain layer** (7 archivos):
- `Permission` (leaf): id, code, name. Método `is(code)`.
- `Role`: id, code, name, permissions[]. Método `can(permissionCode)`.
- `UserAccount`: id, username, email, password, active, roles[]. Método `can(permissionCode)`.
- `GlobalPermissionCode`: enum con 9 códigos. Está en auth/domain/ pero pertenece semánticamente a permissions.
- Repository interfaces como abstract classes (no interfaces) para DI de NestJS.

**Application layer** (6 use cases):
| Use case | Dominio | Repos |
|---|---|---|
| SignUp | users | UserAccountRepository |
| GetUserByCredentials | users | UserAccountRepository |
| CreatePermission | permissions | PermissionRepository |
| GetAllPermissions | permissions | PermissionRepository |
| CreateRole | roles | RoleRepository + PermissionRepository |
| GetAllRoles | roles | RoleRepository |

**Infrastructure layer**:
- 3 controllers en v1/ con sus DTOs
- 6 repos (3 Pg + 3 Memory)
- 3 entities TypeORM con toDomain()/fromDomain()
- AuthGuard necesita UserAccountRepository
- SessionService: independiente, solo JwtService + ConfigService

### Dependencias entre dominios

```
Permission (leaf)
    ↑
Role ─── imports PermissionRepository (CreateRole)
    ↑
UserAccount

AuthGuard ─── imports UserAccountRepository
```

### Opción recomendada: Submódulos anidados bajo auth/

```
src/auth/
  auth.module.ts         ← facade reducido
  permissions/
    permissions.module.ts
    domain/ application/ infrastructure/
  roles/
    roles.module.ts      ← imports PermissionsModule
    domain/ application/ infrastructure/
  users/
    users.module.ts
    domain/ application/ infrastructure/
  session/
    session.module.ts    ← SessionService, AuthGuard, JwtModule
```

### Orden de migración incremental

1. Crear `permissions/` module (sin dependencias)
2. Crear `roles/` module (importa PermissionsModule)
3. Refactorizar `AuthController.me` → `GetUserById` use case (antipatrón)
4. Crear `users/` module
5. Crear `session/` module (SessionService + AuthGuard + JwtModule)
6. Reducir auth.module.ts a facade
7. Mover GlobalPermissionCode a `permissions/domain/`

### Riesgos identificados

1. Imports rotos en ~6 spec files al mover archivos
2. TypeORM entity cross-references — verificar glob de DataSourceOptions.entities cubra nuevas rutas
3. GlobalPermissionCode necesita reubicación y actualizar todos los consumidores
4. AuthController.me bypass de repositorio — corregir antes del refactor
5. Bug latente: PgUserAccountRepository.findByEmailOrUsername usa AND en vez de OR (TypeORM)
6. CreateRole → PermissionRepository: RolesModule debe importar PermissionsModule (acoplamiento manejable)

observations.title: sdd/auth/domain-refactor/proposal
observations.content:
# Proposal: domain-refactor (v2 — post-discussion)

## Intent
El módulo `auth` agrupa tres dominios distintos (permissions, roles, users) más lógica de autenticación JWT en un único módulo NestJS. Además, `AuthController.me` viola la arquitectura hexagonal llamando al repositorio directamente, y `PgUserAccountRepository.findByEmailOrUsername` tiene un bug de AND vs OR. Esta propuesta corrige ambos problemas y reestructura el módulo en submódulos de dominio cohesivos, manteniendo la autenticación en el nivel raíz de `auth/`.

## Estructura destino acordada

```
src/auth/
  auth.module.ts                    ← módulo de autenticación (dueño del flujo auth)
  application/                      ← SignUp, GetUserByCredentials, GetUserById (auth use cases)
  infrastructure/
    controllers/v1/auth.controller.ts
    services/session.service.ts
    guards/auth.guard.ts
    decorators/
  permissions/
    permissions.module.ts
    domain/ application/ infrastructure/
  roles/
    roles.module.ts                 ← imports PermissionsModule
    domain/ application/ infrastructure/
  users/
    users.module.ts
    domain/ application/ infrastructure/
```

**Decisiones acordadas:**
- NO hay submodulo `session/` — SessionService es infraestructura de auth (stateless JWT), no un dominio
- Si en el futuro se agrega invalidación de sesiones, ENTONCES `session/` se crea como subdominio con Session entity + SessionRepository
- AuthController queda en `auth/` root porque maneja autenticación/autorización y crecerá hacia OAuth
- Rate limiting: se agrega como guard en auth.module.ts cuando sea necesario, no requiere cambio estructural

## Scope

### In Scope
- Nuevo use case `GetUserById` + test
- Fix bug `findByEmailOrUsername` AND→OR + test actualizado
- Separar `auth/` en submódulos: `permissions/`, `roles/`, `users/`
- Actualizar todos los imports, registros de módulos y spec files
- Actualizar glob de entities en `migrations.data-source.ts` Y `seed.data-source.ts`
- Mover `GlobalPermissionCode` a `permissions/domain/`
- Mover `permission.decorator.ts` y `session.decorator.ts` a `auth/infrastructure/decorators/` (ya están, se quedan)

### Out of Scope
- Nuevas features o endpoints
- Migraciones de DB o cambios de schema
- Cambios en lógica de domain models
- CI/CD changes
- Session invalidation / refresh tokens (future scope)
- Rate limiting (future scope)

## Phases

| Phase | Description | Constraint |
|-------|-------------|------------|
| 1 | Fix antipatrón: crear `GetUserById`, wirear en `AuthController.me` | Nueva lógica permitida |
| 2 | Fix bug: `PgUserAccountRepository.findByEmailOrUsername` AND→OR | Solo fix TypeORM puntual |
| 3 | Refactor estructural: mover archivos a submódulos, crear módulos, actualizar imports | SOLO mover archivos + actualizar imports. SIN cambios de lógica. |

## Affected Areas

| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `src/auth/application/getUserById.ts` | New | Nuevo use case wrapping UserAccountRepository.findById |
| `src/auth/infrastructure/controllers/v1/auth.controller.ts` | Modified | Inyectar GetUserById en lugar de UserAccountRepository para /me |
| `src/auth/infrastructure/controllers/v1/auth.controller.spec.ts` | Modified | Agregar GetUserById a providers, agregar tests de /me |
| `src/auth/infrastructure/repositories/PgUserAccountRepository.ts` | Modified | Fix where clause AND→OR con array syntax |
| `src/auth/auth.module.ts` | Modified | Importar PermissionsModule, RolesModule, UsersModule |
| `src/auth/permissions/` | New | PermissionsModule + archivos movidos |
| `src/auth/roles/` | New | RolesModule + archivos movidos (importa PermissionsModule) |
| `src/auth/users/` | New | UsersModule + archivos movidos |
| `src/db/migrations.data-source.ts` | Modified | Glob entities: `*` → `**` para cubrir subdirectorios |
| `src/db/seed.data-source.ts` | Modified | Mismo fix de glob |

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| Imports rotos en ~3 spec files después de Fase 3 | High | tsc --noEmit después de cada batch de moves |
| TypeORM entity glob no matchea subdirs anidados | High | Actualizar glob a `**/infrastructure/entities/*` en ambos datasources |
| GlobalPermissionCode relocation rompe consumers | Medium | Grep todos los imports antes de mover |
| CreateRole depende de PermissionRepository cross-module | Medium | PermissionsModule debe exportar PermissionRepository |
| AuthGuard depende de UserAccountRepository cross-module | Medium | auth.module.ts importa UsersModule, AuthGuard usa el repo exportado |

## Rollback Plan
Cada fase es un commit separado. Revertir con `git revert` por fase en orden inverso.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Tests verdes después de Fase 1
- [ ] Tests verdes después de Fase 2 (OR verificado con test dedicado)
- [ ] Tests verdes después de Fase 3
- [ ] `tsc --noEmit` pasa sin errores después de cada fase
- [ ] Sin acceso directo a repositorios desde controllers
- [ ] Entity glob resuelve las 3 entities en ambos datasources después del refactor

observations.title: sdd/auth/domain-refactor/design
observations.content:
# Technical Design: domain-refactor

## Module Dependency Graph (final state)

```
AppModule
  imports: [ConfigModule, TypeOrmModule.forRoot, AuthModule, PermissionsModule, RolesModule]

AuthModule
  imports: [JwtModule.registerAsync, UsersModule]
  controllers: [AuthController]
  providers: [SignUp, GetUserByCredentials, GetUserById, SessionService, AuthGuard]
  exports: [AuthGuard, SessionService]

UsersModule
  imports: []
  controllers: []
  providers: [{ provide: UserAccountRepository, useClass: PgUserAccountRepository }]
  exports: [UserAccountRepository]

PermissionsModule
  imports: [AuthModule]
  controllers: [PermissionController]
  providers: [{ provide: PermissionRepository, useClass: PgPermissionRepository }, CreatePermission, GetAllPermissions]
  exports: [PermissionRepository]

RolesModule
  imports: [AuthModule, PermissionsModule]
  controllers: [RoleController]
  providers: [{ provide: RoleRepository, useClass: PgRoleRepository }, CreateRole, GetAllRoles]
  exports: [RoleRepository]
```

**DAG — no circular dependencies:**
```
UsersModule → (nada)
AuthModule → UsersModule, JwtModule
PermissionsModule → AuthModule
RolesModule → AuthModule, PermissionsModule
AppModule → todos
```

## Phase 1: GetUserById use case

**New file**: `src/auth/application/getUserById.ts`
```typescript
@Injectable()
export class GetUserById {
  constructor(private readonly userAccountRepository: UserAccountRepository) {}
  async execute(id: string): Promise<UserAccount> {
    const userAccount = await this.userAccountRepository.findById(id);
    if (!userAccount) throw new UnauthorizedException();
    return userAccount;
  }
}
```

**AuthController.me — before:**
```typescript
const userAccount = await this.userAccountRepository.findById(session?.userId ?? '');
if (!userAccount) throw new UnauthorizedException();
```

**AuthController.me — after:**
```typescript
const userAccount = await this.getUserByIdUseCase.execute(session?.userId ?? '');
```

**Constructor change**: quitar `userAccountRepository`, agregar `getUserByIdUseCase: GetUserById`.

**auth.module.ts**: agregar `GetUserById` a providers.

**Tests nuevos:**
- `src/auth/application/getUserById.spec.ts` — unit test: found/not found
- `src/auth/infrastructure/controllers/v1/auth.controller.spec.ts` — agregar `GetUserById` a providers, agregar describe('me')

## Phase 2: Fix findByEmailOrUsername

**Bug**: `findOne({ where: { email, username } })` genera AND. Debe ser OR.

**Fix**:
```typescript
const foundUserAccount = await this.dataSource
  .getRepository(UserAccountEntity)
  .findOne({ where: [{ email }, { username }] });
```

**Test**: `src/auth/infrastructure/repositories/MemoryUserAccountRepository.spec.ts` — verificar OR semántico.

**También**: alinear type signature a `Partial<Pick<...>>` en ambas implementaciones.

## Phase 3: Migración estructural incremental

### Orden
1. PermissionsModule → 2. RolesModule → 3. UsersModule → 4. AuthModule refactor

### 3.1 PermissionsModule — archivos a mover

| From | To |
|------|----|
| `auth/domain/Permission.ts` | `auth/permissions/domain/Permission.ts` |
| `auth/domain/PermissionRepository.ts` | `auth/permissions/domain/PermissionRepository.ts` |
| `auth/domain/GlobalPermissionCode.ts` | `auth/permissions/domain/GlobalPermissionCode.ts` |
| `auth/application/createPermission.ts` | `auth/permissions/application/createPermission.ts` |
| `auth/application/getAllPermissions.ts` | `auth/permissions/application/getAllPermissions.ts` |
| `auth/infrastructure/controllers/v1/permission.controller.ts` | `auth/permissions/infrastructure/controllers/v1/permission.controller.ts` |
| `auth/infrastructure/controllers/v1/permission.controller.spec.ts` | `auth/permissions/infrastructure/controllers/v1/permission.controller.spec.ts` |
| `auth/infrastructure/controllers/v1/dtos/CreatePermissionDto.ts` | `auth/permissions/infrastructure/controllers/v1/dtos/CreatePermissionDto.ts` |
| `auth/infrastructure/repositories/PgPermissionRepository.ts` | `auth/permissions/infrastructure/repositories/PgPermissionRepository.ts` |
| `auth/infrastructure/repositories/MemoryPermissionRepository.ts` | `auth/permissions/infrastructure/repositories/MemoryPermissionRepository.ts` |
| `auth/infrastructure/entities/PermissionEntity.ts` | `auth/permissions/infrastructure/entities/PermissionEntity.ts` |

**Imports que hay que actualizar:**
- `Role.ts` → Permission path
- `RoleEntity.ts` → PermissionEntity path
- `auth.guard.ts` → PermissionAttributes, UserAccountRepository
- `permission.decorator.ts` → PermissionPrimitive

### 3.2 RolesModule — archivos a mover

| From | To |
|------|----|
| `auth/domain/Role.ts` | `auth/roles/domain/Role.ts` |
| `auth/domain/RoleRepository.ts` | `auth/roles/domain/RoleRepository.ts` |
| `auth/application/createRole.ts` | `auth/roles/application/createRole.ts` |
| `auth/application/getAllRoles.ts` | `auth/roles/application/getAllRoles.ts` |
| `auth/infrastructure/controllers/v1/role.controller.ts` | `auth/roles/infrastructure/controllers/v1/role.controller.ts` |
| `auth/infrastructure/controllers/v1/role.controller.spec.ts` | `auth/roles/infrastructure/controllers/v1/role.controller.spec.ts` |
| `auth/infrastructure/controllers/v1/dtos/CreateRoleDto.ts` | `auth/roles/infrastructure/controllers/v1/dtos/CreateRoleDto.ts` |
| `auth/infrastructure/repositories/PgRoleRepository.ts` | `auth/roles/infrastructure/repositories/PgRoleRepository.ts` |
| `auth/infrastructure/repositories/MemoryRoleRepository.ts` | `auth/roles/infrastructure/repositories/MemoryRoleRepository.ts` |
| `auth/infrastructure/entities/RoleEntity.ts` | `auth/roles/infrastructure/entities/RoleEntity.ts` |

**RolesModule imports PermissionsModule** para que CreateRole pueda inyectar PermissionRepository.

### 3.3 UsersModule — archivos a mover

| From | To |
|------|----|
| `auth/domain/UserAccount.ts` | `auth/users/domain/UserAccount.ts` |
| `auth/domain/UserAccountRepository.ts` | `auth/users/domain/UserAccountRepository.ts` |
| `auth/infrastructure/repositories/PgUserAccountRepository.ts` | `auth/users/infrastructure/repositories/PgUserAccountRepository.ts` |
| `auth/infrastructure/repositories/MemoryUserAccountRepository.ts` | `auth/users/infrastructure/repositories/MemoryUserAccountRepository.ts` |
| `auth/infrastructure/entities/UserAccountEntity.ts` | `auth/users/infrastructure/entities/UserAccountEntity.ts` |

### 3.4 AuthModule final

```typescript
@Module({
  imports: [JwtModule.registerAsync({...}), UsersModule],
  controllers: [AuthController],
  providers: [SignUp, GetUserByCredentials, GetUserById, SessionService, AuthGuard],
  exports: [AuthGuard, SessionService],
})
```

### 3.5 Entity glob

**Ambos** `migrations.data-source.ts` y `seed.data-source.ts`:
```typescript
// Antes:
entities: [__dirname + '/../*/infrastructure/entities/*{.ts,.js}']
// Después:
entities: [__dirname + '/../**/infrastructure/entities/*{.ts,.js}']
```

## Archivos con imports absolutos que se rompen en Fase 3

| Archivo | Import a actualizar |
|---------|---------------------|
| `auth.guard.ts` | `src/auth/domain/Permission` → `src/auth/permissions/domain/Permission` |
| `auth.guard.ts` | `src/auth/domain/UserAccountRepository` → `src/auth/users/domain/UserAccountRepository` |
| `permission.decorator.ts` | `src/auth/domain/Permission` → `src/auth/permissions/domain/Permission` |
| `UserAccountEntity.ts` | `src/auth/domain/UserAccount` → `src/auth/users/domain/UserAccount` |

## Decisiones de diseño clave

1. **AuthModule NO importa PermissionsModule/RolesModule** — evita circular deps. Los submodulos importan AuthModule.
2. **UsersModule sin controllers** — use cases de auth (SignUp, etc.) quedan en auth/application/ porque son orquestación de autenticación.
3. **GetUserById lanza UnauthorizedException** (no NotFoundException) — mantiene comportamiento actual del me endpoint.
4. **seed.data-source.ts hereda DataSourceOptions** de migrations — actualizar solo uno es suficiente.

observations.title: sdd/auth/domain-refactor/tasks
observations.content:
# Tasks: domain-refactor (v2 — post-discusión Phase 2)

## Phase 1: Fix antipatron GetUserById

- 1.1 TEST: crear `src/auth/application/getUserById.spec.ts` (unit: found / UnauthorizedException)
- 1.2 IMPL: crear `src/auth/application/getUserById.ts` (@Injectable, execute(id) → UserAccount | throws UnauthorizedException)
- 1.3 TEST: actualizar `auth.controller.spec.ts` — agregar GetUserById a providers + describe('me') con 2 casos
- 1.4 IMPL: refactorizar `auth.controller.ts` — quitar userAccountRepository del constructor, usar getUserByIdUseCase.execute
- 1.5 IMPL: actualizar `auth.module.ts` — agregar GetUserById a providers
- 1.6 GATE: `pnpm tsc --noEmit && pnpm test`

Dependencias: 1.1→1.2→1.3→1.4→1.5→1.6

## Phase 2: Refactor UserAccountRepository — findByEmail + findByUsername

**Decisión acordada**: eliminar `findByEmailOrUsername`, reemplazar por dos métodos separados `findByEmail` y `findByUsername`. Login solo por email. SignUp chequea ambos por separado (dos queries).

**Nota de deuda técnica**: sin índices en email/username, las queries son O(n). No agregar índices — eso es scope futuro.

- 2.1 TEST: actualizar/crear tests para `MemoryUserAccountRepository`:
  - `findByEmail`: found / not found
  - `findByUsername`: found / not found
- 2.2 IMPL: actualizar `UserAccountRepository` (abstract class) — eliminar `findByEmailOrUsername`, agregar `findByEmail(email: string)` y `findByUsername(username: string)`
- 2.3 IMPL: actualizar `PgUserAccountRepository` — eliminar `findByEmailOrUsername`, implementar `findByEmail` con `findOne({ where: { email } })` y `findByUsername` con `findOne({ where: { username } })`
- 2.4 IMPL: actualizar `MemoryUserAccountRepository` — idem con lógica de array, eliminar `findByEmailOrUsername`
- 2.5 IMPL: actualizar `GetUserByCredentials` — reemplazar `findByEmailOrUsername({ email })` por `findByEmail(email)`
- 2.6 IMPL: actualizar `SignUp` — reemplazar `findByEmailOrUsername({ email, username })` por dos llamadas: `findByEmail(email)` + `findByUsername(username)`, si cualquiera retorna algo lanza BadRequestException
- 2.7 GATE: `pnpm tsc --noEmit && pnpm test`

Dependencias: 2.1→2.2→2.3,2.4→2.5,2.6→2.7 (independiente de Phase 1)

## Phase 3: Migracion estructural (SOLO mover + imports, SIN lógica)

Requiere: gates 1.6 y 2.7 pasados.

### 3.1 PermissionsModule
- 3.1.1 MOVE: 11 archivos a `src/auth/permissions/`
- 3.1.2 CREATE: `src/auth/permissions/permissions.module.ts` (imports: [AuthModule], exports: [PermissionRepository])
- 3.1.3 IMPORTS: actualizar Role.ts, RoleEntity.ts, auth.guard.ts, permission.decorator.ts, archivos movidos internos
- 3.1.4 MODULE: auth.module.ts — quitar PermissionController, CreatePermission, GetAllPermissions, PermissionRepository
- 3.1.5 GATE: tsc + test

### 3.2 RolesModule
- 3.2.1 MOVE: 10 archivos a `src/auth/roles/`
- 3.2.2 CREATE: `src/auth/roles/roles.module.ts` (imports: [AuthModule, PermissionsModule], exports: [RoleRepository])
- 3.2.3 IMPORTS: actualizar UserAccount.ts, UserAccountEntity.ts, archivos movidos internos
- 3.2.4 MODULE: auth.module.ts — quitar RoleController, CreateRole, GetAllRoles, RoleRepository
- 3.2.5 GATE: tsc + test

### 3.3 UsersModule
- 3.3.1 MOVE: 5 archivos a `src/auth/users/`
- 3.3.2 CREATE: `src/auth/users/users.module.ts` (imports: [], exports: [UserAccountRepository])
- 3.3.3 IMPORTS: actualizar signUp.ts, getUserByCredentials.ts, getUserById.ts, auth.guard.ts, auth.controller.spec.ts, archivos movidos internos
- 3.3.4 GATE: tsc + test

### 3.4 AuthModule final
- 3.4.1 MODULE: auth.module.ts → imports: [JwtModule, UsersModule], exports: [AuthGuard, SessionService]
- 3.4.2 MODULE: app.module.ts — agregar PermissionsModule y RolesModule a imports

### 3.5 Entity glob
- 3.5.1 FIX: migrations.data-source.ts — `/../*/infrastructure` → `/../**/infrastructure`
- 3.5.2 VERIFY: seed.data-source.ts — confirmar herencia de DataSourceOptions sin override de entities

### 3.6 Limpieza
- 3.6.1 DELETE: `src/auth/domain/` (vacío tras moves)
- 3.6.2 VERIFY: `src/auth/infrastructure/` — solo quedan auth controller, decorators, guards, services

### 3.7 GATE FINAL: tsc + test

## Deuda técnica anotada
- Sin índices en email/username — queries O(n). Agregar índices cuando el volumen lo justifique.
- Si a futuro se agrega login por username, agregar caso en GetUserByCredentials usando findByUsername.

observations.title: sdd-init/go-cli
observations.content:
**What**: SDD initialized for go-cli — new Go CLI application with TUI, empty project directory.

**Why**: New project kickoff. User wants a Go CLI app with Bubbletea TUI and shell-agnostic wrapper scripts.

**Stack**:
- Language: Go (to be initialized with go mod)
- TUI: Bubbletea (github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea)
- Shell wrappers: Bash + Zsh scripts wrapping the Go binary
- Config: likely ~/.config/go-cli/ or XDG base dirs

**Architecture intent**:
- CLI binary in Go with subcommands (e.g. `gp`)
- Shell output scripts: Go binary outputs shell commands (cd, export, etc.) that parent shell evaluates via `eval $(gp ...)`
- TUI: interactive fuzzy-filter list for project selection (Bubbletea)
- Tab autocomplete: shell completion scripts (bash-completion, zsh compdef)
- Multi-shell compatibility: Bash + Zsh wrappers
- MCP-ready architecture: commands must be usable as MCP tools in the future — clean interface, JSON-friendly output modes
- `gp` command: manages configured project directories (name → path), opens TUI if no args

**Key constraints**:
- Shell cd limitation: a child process cannot change the parent shell's directory — must use eval pattern
- Autocomplete must work in both bash and zsh
- TUI must support fuzzy input filtering for large project lists
- Architecture must be flexible for future MCP server integration (no MCP implementation now)

**Where**: /Users/arielsurco/projects/go-cli (empty — not yet initialized)

**Learned**:
- Shell cd requires eval pattern: `eval $(gp my-pro)` or sourcing a function in .bashrc/.zshrc
- Go binary outputs the cd command as text, shell wrapper evaluates it
- For MCP readiness: keep command I/O clean (stdin/stdout), support --json flag pattern

observations.title: Shell wrapper strategy — eval vs functions
observations.content:
**What**: Shell wrappers use simple functions by default. `eval` only for `dev` command.

**Why**: `dev` needs eval because custom project scripts may need to set env vars (`export`, `PATH` modifications) in the parent shell. Other commands (like `gp`) only need `cd` — shell function is safer, simpler, and avoids injection risk.

**Rule**: Avoid eval unless the command MUST modify parent shell environment state (exports, PATH). `cd` alone never justifies eval.

**Where**: Shell wrapper scripts for bash and zsh (.bashrc / .zshrc sourced functions)

**Learned**:
- `gp` → shell function, Go prints path, wrapper does `cd "$dir"`
- `dev` → shell function with eval, Go prints path + script, wrapper does `cd "$dir" && eval "$script"`
- Any new command: default to NO eval, justify explicitly if needed

observations.title: TTY detection — TUI vs clean output mode
observations.content:
**What**: App detects TTY to switch between interactive TUI and clean parseable output.

**Why**: Enables Cobra + Fang + Bubbletea hybrid. Also provides MCP-readiness without implementing MCP now — future MCP server invokes binary in non-interactive mode and consumes clean stdout.

**Where**: All commands that have both interactive and scriptable use cases

**Learned**:
- `term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))` is the Go idiom
- TTY → launch Bubbletea TUI
- Pipe/redirect → clean stdout (path, JSON, etc.)
- Fang handles color profile detection for the Cobra layer automatically

observations.title: Project constraints — TDD, linter, wrappers, Homebrew
observations.content:
**What**: Non-negotiable constraints for all development on go-cli.

**Why**: User defined these upfront — apply to every command implemented from here forward.

**Constraints**:

1. **TDD**: Write tests first. No code without a failing test first.

2. **Linter**: golangci-lint with near-default config. Keep it simple to avoid friction when user edits code manually. No overly strict rules.

3. **Shell wrappers**: Every command that needs to modify parent shell state (cd, export, etc.) MUST have a bash wrapper AND a zsh wrapper. Autocomplete where applicable (e.g. `gp` needs project name completion).

4. **Homebrew install**: Must be installable via `brew install` from the GitHub repo URL (custom tap or goreleaser + homebrew-tap). Single command install on macOS and Linux. Same for upgrade (`brew upgrade`).
   - Likely approach: GoReleaser + GitHub Actions to publish releases + Homebrew tap auto-updated on release.
   - Fallback: `brew install --HEAD` from repo URL if tap is overkill early on.

5. **Applies to ALL commands**: Every new command added must follow these 4 constraints automatically.

**Learned**:
- GoReleaser is the standard for Go CLI Homebrew distribution
- Homebrew on Linux = Linuxbrew, same tap works
- Shell wrappers should be distributed as part of the install (Homebrew formula copies them to the right place)

```
go-cli/
  cmd/           # Cobra wiring only, no business logic

```

## Config
- Path: `~/.config/go-cli/config.toml` via `github.com/adrg/xdg`
- Format: TOML (`github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2`)
- Structure: `[[projects]]` with name, path, dev_script

## Autocomplete
- Cobra `ValidArgsFunction` — loads config at TAB-time, returns project names
- `generate_completions_from_executable` in GoReleaser handles distribution

## Shell Wrappers
- Distributed in release archive, installed to Homebrew `libexec`
- Caveats block prints sourcing instructions (unavoidable — no auto-source possible)
- gp.bash: `gp() { local dir; dir=$(go-cli gp "$@") && cd "$dir"; }`
- dev.bash: `dev() { local dir script; dir=$(go-cli dev --print-dir "$@"); script=$(go-cli dev --print-script "$@"); cd "$dir" && eval "$script"; }`

## GoReleaser
- Use `homebrew_casks` key (NOT `brews` — deprecated)
- Tap repo: `homebrew-go-cli`
- GitHub Actions: on push tag v*, needs HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN PAT with repo write scope

## TUI Fuzzy Filter
- `bubbles/list` has built-in fuzzy filter (sahilm/fuzzy), press `/` to enter filter mode
- Items implement `FilterValue() string`
- No separate textinput needed for filtering

## golangci-lint
- `default: standard` + revive, misspell, nolintlint, gocritic
- Disable exported doc enforcement
- Line length 120, complexity 15

## teatest
- Import: `github.com/charmbracelet/x/exp/teatest`
- Keep config loading in tea.Cmd so tests inject via tm.Send() without touching filesystem

## Risks
1. GoReleaser homebrew_casks vs brews (use casks)
2. teatest import path — verify before writing tests
3. Shell wrapper sourcing requires user action (unavoidable)
4. Binary name conflict if using short name

## Open Questions
1. Binary name: `gp` or `go-cli`?
2. Config global-only or also project-local?
3. `dev` command eval: always or only when needed?

observations.title: sdd/go-cli/proposal
observations.content:
# Architecture Proposal: arielsurco-cli

## Binary & Stack
- Binary: `arielsurco-cli`
- Go + Cobra + Fang v2 (`charm.land/fang/v2`) + Bubbletea v2 + bubbles/list + Lip Gloss v2
- Config: TOML via go-toml/v2, paths via github.com/adrg/xdg
- Linter: golangci-lint (default: standard + revive, misspell, nolintlint, gocritic)
- Testing: TDD, go test, teatest for TUI

## Project Structure
```
cmd/root.go, cmd/setup.go, cmd/shellinit.go, cmd/project/{go,dev,add,remove}.go
internal/config/, internal/module/, internal/project/, internal/tui/{projectlist,setup}/, internal/shell/
```

## Module System
- Registry in internal/module/registry.go — Go struct per module with wrapper templates compiled-in
- CommandDef has NeedsEval bool (only gpd=true)
- Adding future module = add entry to Registry + cmd files

## Alias naming
- gp = go project | gpd = go project dev | gpa = go project add | gpr = go project remove
- Future: opensearch → o prefix, sentry → s prefix

## Config Precedence
.arielsurco-cli.toml (project-local) > ~/.config/arielsurco-cli/config.toml (global) > offer to create

## Shell wrappers — generated at runtime
- eval "$(arielsurco-cli shell-init)" — one line in .zshrc/.bashrc
- shell-init reads active.toml, emits wrappers from compiled-in registry
- eval ONLY for gpd (dev scripts may export env vars)
- No static wrapper files — generated from registry, always matches binary version

## Command tree
arielsurco-cli project go|dev|add|remove + setup + shell-init

## TTY detection
term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd())) → TTY: Bubbletea TUI | non-TTY: clean stdout (MCP-ready)

## Install
GoReleaser + homebrew_casks tap (arielsurco/homebrew-arielsurco-cli)
brew install arielsurco/arielsurco-cli/arielsurco-cli + arielsurco-cli setup

## Rollout phases
1. Foundation: config+project CRUD+gpa+gpr
2. Navigation: gp TTY+non-TTY+autocomplete
3. Dev: gpd both modes
4. Shell integration: module registry+generator+shell-init
5. Setup TUI
6. Release: GitHub Actions+tap+v0.1.0

## Risks
- Bubbletea v2 is beta — pin exact version
- homebrew_casks macOS-oriented — Linux via GitHub releases
- gpd eval is controlled (Go constructs the command, not raw user input)

# Spec: arielsurco-cli — DELTA (completions update)

## CORRECTION to Phase 4: Shell Integration

### R4.3 — shell-init (updated, replaces previous)
- MUST emit tab completion wiring for every alias that has a corresponding Cobra subcommand.
- Completion MUST work via Cobra's hidden `__complete` subcommand called at TAB-time.
- `CommandDef` MUST include `HasCompletions bool` — true for commands that accept project name argument (`gp`, `gpd`); false for commands with no completable args (`gpa`, `gpr`).

### R4.4 — Bash completion wiring (MUST)
For each CommandDef with HasCompletions=true, shell-init MUST emit:
```bash
_<alias>_completions() {
  local cur="${COMP_WORDS[COMP_CWORD]}"
  COMPREPLY=($(arielsurco-cli __complete <cobra-cmd> "$cur" 2>/dev/null | grep -v '^:'))
}
complete -F _<alias>_completions <alias>
```

### R4.5 — Zsh completion wiring (MUST)
For each CommandDef with HasCompletions=true, shell-init MUST emit:
```zsh
_<alias>() {
  local completions=($(arielsurco-cli __complete <cobra-cmd> "${words[2]}" 2>/dev/null | grep -v '^:'))
  _describe 'project' completions
}
compdef _<alias> <alias>
```

### R4.6 — Completion accuracy
- Completions MUST reflect the current config at TAB-time (not cached).
- Cobra's ValidArgsFunction for `project go` and `project dev` MUST return project names.
- `ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp` MUST be set to suppress file completion fallback.

### R4.7 — Golden files (updated)
- Golden files MUST include both function definition AND completion wiring.
- `internal/shell/testdata/golden_bash.txt` and `golden_zsh.txt` updated accordingly.

## Updated CommandDef table
| Alias | CobraCmd      | NeedsEval | HasCompletions |

| gpd   | project dev  | true      | true           |
| gpa   | project add  | false     | false          |

- AC4.6: After sourcing shell-init output, `gpd <TAB>` returns project names from config.

observations.title: sdd/go-cli/design
observations.content:
# Technical Design: arielsurco-cli (updated — completions added)

## Package interfaces
- config: Load()/Save()/LoadActive()/SaveActive() — leaf package, zero internal deps
- project: Service struct with Add/Remove/FindByName/List/DevCommand — deps: config only
- module: Registry []Module (compiled-in), FindModule/ActiveModules — leaf, zero internal deps
- shell: IsTerminal()/DetectShell()/Generate() — deps: module, golang.org/x/term
- tui/projectlist: New(projects, preFilter)/Result() — deps: config, bubbletea v1, bubbles/list
- tui/setup: New(allModules, currentActive)/Result() — deps: module, bubbletea v1

## Dependency graph (DAG, no cycles)
cmd/* -> config, module, project(->config), shell(->module), tui/projectlist(->config), tui/setup(->module)

## CommandDef struct (updated)
```go
type CommandDef struct {
    Alias          string
    CobraCmd       string  // e.g. "project go"
    NeedsEval      bool
    HasCompletions bool    // true = emit completion wiring in shell-init
    Description    string
}
```

| Alias | CobraCmd       | NeedsEval | HasCompletions |
|-------|---------------|-----------|----------------|
| gp    | project go    | false     | true           |
| gpd   | project dev   | true      | true           |
| gpa   | project add   | false     | false          |
| gpr   | project remove| false     | false          |

## Generate() — updated (3 output blocks per command with completions)

For each CommandDef, Generate() emits:

1. Function block (always):
   - NeedsEval=false: `function <alias>() { arielsurco-cli <cobra-cmd> "$@" }`
   - NeedsEval=true: `function <alias>() { eval "$(arielsurco-cli <cobra-cmd> "$@")" }`

2. Completion helper (if HasCompletions=true):
   Bash:
   ```
   _<alias>_completions() {
     local cur="${COMP_WORDS[COMP_CWORD]}"
     COMPREPLY=($(arielsurco-cli __complete <cobra-cmd> "$cur" 2>/dev/null | grep -v '^:'))
   }
   complete -F _<alias>_completions <alias>
   ```
   Zsh:
   ```
   _<alias>() {
     local completions=($(arielsurco-cli __complete <cobra-cmd> "${words[2]}" 2>/dev/null | grep -v '^:'))
     _describe 'project' completions
   }
   compdef _<alias> <alias>
   ```

## How tab completion works at runtime
1. User types: `gp <TAB>`
2. Shell invokes the registered completion function `_gp` / `_gp_completions`
3. That function calls: `arielsurco-cli __complete project go "" 2>/dev/null`
4. Cobra's __complete handler loads config, runs ValidArgsFunction, returns project names
5. Shell presents completions — always reflects current config, never cached

## Cobra ValidArgsFunction (gp and gpd)
```go
ValidArgsFunction: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string, toComplete string) ([]cobra.Completion, cobra.ShellCompDirective) {
    cfg, err := config.Load()
    if err != nil { return nil, cobra.ShellCompDirectiveError }
    completions := make([]cobra.Completion, len(cfg.Projects))
    for i, p := range cfg.Projects { completions[i] = p.Name }
    return completions, cobra.ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp
}
```

## Key design decisions
1. Wrappers compiled into binary (not files) — version coherence, zero file management
2. NeedsEval bool + HasCompletions bool in CommandDef — drives Generate() output
3. Generate() uses fmt.Sprintf format strings — no text/template needed
4. Completion calls __complete at TAB-time — always fresh from config, zero caching
5. Service does NOT call config.Save() — Cobra command layer handles persistence
6. TUI models never do I/O — data injected at construction, result read from FinalModel()
7. Sentinel errors in internal/project/errors.go — cmd layer adds user-facing context
8. Atomic save: write tempfile in same dir + os.Rename (POSIX atomic)
9. Config loaded once per invocation, XDG path via adrg/xdg, never hardcoded ~/.config

## TTY decision tree (gp/gpd)
- IsTerminal=true + args: TUI with preFilter seed
- IsTerminal=true + no args: TUI empty filter
- IsTerminal=false + args: direct lookup, print to stdout
- IsTerminal=false + no args: ErrNameRequired, exit 1

## Config merge algorithm
Load global (missing=empty ok) → detect .arielsurco-cli.toml in CWD → merge local.dev_script by name match → return

## GoReleaser
- homebrew_casks (NOT brews — deprecated)
- 4 builds: darwin+linux × amd64+arm64
- ldflags: -X cmd.Version={{.Version}}
- GitHub Actions: on push v*.*.*, needs TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN

## Testing
- config/project/module: unit tests with t.TempDir() + t.Setenv XDG_CONFIG_HOME
- shell: golden file tests (testdata/golden_*.txt with function + completion wiring), -update flag
- tui/*: teatest, inject messages, assert FinalModel().Result()
- Acceptance: after sourcing shell-init, `gp <TAB>` returns project names

observations.title: Backlog — alias customization TUI (post v0.1.0)
observations.content:
**What**: Allow users to customize shell alias names via a TUI (e.g. rename `gp` to `goto`).

observations.title: Naming conventions — no single-letter variables
observations.content:
**What**: All variables and function parameters must have descriptive names. No single-letter variables except loop indices in very short scopes.

**Why**: User reads code without AI assistance. Single-letter names like `p`, `s`, `v`, `i`, `lc`, `am`, `tmp` are not self-explanatory.

**Rules**:
- Receivers: `svc` instead of `s`, descriptive over abbreviated
- Loop variables: `existingProject` not `p`, `index` not `i`
- Generic params: `result` not `v`
- Local config: `override` not `lc`
- Active modules params: `activeModules` not `am`
- Temp files: `tempFile`/`tempFilePath` not `tmp`/`tmpName`
- Shell commands in tests: `shellCommand` not `cmd` (also avoids cobra.Command collision)
- Found items: `foundProject` not `p`
- Expected values in tests: `expectedProject`, `expectedCommand`, etc.

**Applies to**: ALL phases going forward — enforce in sdd-apply prompts.

observations.title: Atomic commits per phase
observations.content:
**What**: Each phase must be committed atomically with a descriptive conventional commit message.

**Why**: User wants clean git history. Phase 1+2 were forgiven as a single init commit, but from Phase 3 onwards each phase gets its own commit.

**How to apply**: At the end of every sdd-apply phase, run git add + git commit with a conventional commit message describing what the phase implemented. Use `feat:` for new features.

- Login solo por email (`GetUserByCredentials` usa `findByEmail`)
- SignUp chequea email Y username por separado (dos queries)
- `MemoryUserAccountRepository` sigue el patrón: `Promise.resolve(foundUserAccount ? foundUserAccount : null)`
- Tests: `MemoryUserAccountRepository.spec.ts` con 4 casos

**Phase 3 — Refactor estructural**
- Módulos de dominio extraídos al nivel `src/`: `src/permissions/`, `src/roles/`, `src/users/`
- `src/auth/` quedó como módulo de autenticación puro (guards, session, use cases de auth)
- Controllers centralizados en `src/api/v1/{concern}/` con DTOs en `src/api/v1/{concern}/dtos/`
- `AppModule` solo importa `ApiModule` + infraestructura global
- Entity glob actualizado a `**/infrastructure/entities/` para cubrir subdirectorios anidados

## Key decisions

1. **`AuthModule` exporta `UsersModule`** — resuelve dependencias transitivas de `AuthGuard` en módulos que importan `AuthModule`
2. **Sin session/ submodule** — `SessionService` es infraestructura stateless de JWT, vive en `auth/infrastructure/services/`
3. **Controllers como adapters HTTP** — separados completamente de los dominios, centralizados en `ApiModule`
4. **DTOs co-ubicados con controllers** — `src/api/v1/{concern}/dtos/` para ownership claro
5. **`noImplicitAny: true`** — activado en tsconfig; resolvió los falsos positivos de ESLint

## Module graph

```
AppModule → ApiModule
ApiModule → AuthModule, PermissionsModule, RolesModule
AuthModule → UsersModule | exports: [AuthGuard, SessionService, UsersModule, SignUp, GetUserByCredentials, GetUserById]
UsersModule → (nada) | exports: [UserAccountRepository]
PermissionsModule → AuthModule | exports: [PermissionRepository, CreatePermission, GetAllPermissions]
RolesModule → AuthModule, PermissionsModule | exports: [RoleRepository, CreateRole, GetAllRoles]
```

## Tech debt

- Sin índices en `email` y `username` en `UserAccountEntity` — queries O(n). Agregar cuando el volumen lo justifique
- Login por username no implementado — si se agrega, usar `findByUsername` en `GetUserByCredentials`
- `session/` submodule no existe — crearlo cuando se necesite invalidación de sesiones (Session entity + SessionRepository)
- Rate limiting pendiente — agregar como guard en `AuthModule` cuando sea necesario

## Files

- `src/auth/application/getUserById.ts` — nuevo use case
- `src/auth/application/getUserById.spec.ts` — tests
- `src/users/domain/UserAccountRepository.ts` — interface refactorizada
- `src/users/infrastructure/repositories/PgUserAccountRepository.ts` — implementación Pg
- `src/users/infrastructure/repositories/MemoryUserAccountRepository.ts` — implementación Memory
- `src/auth/auth.module.ts` — módulo de autenticación final
- `src/permissions/permissions.module.ts` — nuevo módulo
- `src/roles/roles.module.ts` — nuevo módulo
- `src/users/users.module.ts` — nuevo módulo
- `src/api/api.module.ts` — nuevo módulo centralizador de HTTP
- `src/app.module.ts` — root module simplificado
- `src/db/migrations.data-source.ts` — glob actualizado

observations.title: Session summary: auth
observations.content:
## Goal
Refactor completo del proyecto NestJS auth: separar dominios, centralizar API, corregir antipatrones y documentar convenciones para otras IAs.

## Instructions
- NO cambiar lógica sin consultar primero — solo moves + imports en refactors estructurales
- Conventional commits sin AI attribution
- NO hacer build (nest build) después de cambios
- Formato de codes: siempre lowercase dot-separated (global.permissions.create), no UPPERCASE_UNDERSCORE
- Cuando hay dudas de diseño, presentar opciones con tradeoffs antes de implementar
- El usuario trabaja con Claude Code, Cursor y Copilot — skills en .agent/skills/ para compatibilidad universal

## Discoveries
- AuthModule debe re-exportar UsersModule para que AuthGuard resuelva UserAccountRepository transitivamente en PermissionsModule y RolesModule
- noImplicitAny: false + recommendedTypeChecked ESLint genera falsos positivos de no-unsafe-assignment — solución: activar noImplicitAny: true
- sdd-apply puede malformar código (signUp.ts quedó con self-reference y throw incondicional) — siempre verificar manualmente archivos tocados por agentes
- TypeORM findOne({ where: { email, username } }) genera AND, no OR — usar array [{ email }, { username }] para OR
- Entity glob **/infrastructure/entities necesita ** (doble asterisco) para cubrir subdirectorios anidados
- MemoryUserAccountRepository debe usar Promise.resolve(found ? found : null) — no ?? null ni return directo
- UUID mock global retorna siempre 'mock-uuid-v4' — crear múltiples entidades en el mismo test requiere IDs explícitos

## Accomplished
- ✅ SDD completo: explore → propose → design → tasks → apply → verify → archive para domain-refactor
- ✅ Phase 1: GetUserById use case — AuthController.me ya no accede directo al repositorio
- ✅ Phase 2: findByEmailOrUsername eliminado → findByEmail + findByUsername separados; login solo por email
- ✅ Phase 3: módulos extraídos a src/permissions/, src/roles/, src/users/ (nivel raíz, no anidados en auth/)
- ✅ Controllers centralizados en src/api/v1/{concern}/ con DTOs en dtos/ subfolder
- ✅ noImplicitAny: true activado en tsconfig

## Relevant Files

- src/users/domain/UserAccountRepository.ts — interface refactorizada (findByEmail + findByUsername)

observations.title: Tests that call config.Save() need XDG isolation
observations.content:
**What**: Any test that transitively calls config.Save() MUST have t.Setenv("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", t.TempDir()) to isolate it from the real user config.

**Why**: Tests using in-memory *config.Config structs still call GlobalConfigPath() → xdg.Reload() → real ~/.config path when Save() is called. This polluted the real config during testing (project "other" appeared after go test).

**How to apply**: Before adding any test that calls a function which eventually calls config.Save() or config.SaveActive(), add t.Setenv("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", t.TempDir()) at the top of the test.

**Affected functions**: removeByName, runRemoveWithTerminalState (exact match paths), any future command that mutates config.

observations.content:
# Verify Report: arielsurco-cli v0.1.0

## Status: PASS

## CRITICAL
- [PASS] All tests pass — `go test ./...` green across 9 packages (14 test files)
- [PASS] Linter clean — `golangci-lint run` reports 0 issues
- [PASS] GoReleaser valid — `goreleaser check` passes
- [PASS] Config atomic save — `atomicSaveTOML()` uses `os.CreateTemp` + `os.Rename` (internal/config/config.go:133-161)
- [PASS] XDG path resolution — uses `adrg/xdg` with `xdg.Reload()` before each path read; no hardcoded `~/.config`
- [PASS] Shell wrappers CdOutput/NeedsEval — `gp` uses CdOutput=true (captures output, cd into it); `gpd` uses NeedsEval=true (eval wrapper); `gpa`/`gpr` use direct call
- [PASS] Tab completions wired — `gp` and `gpd` have HasCompletions=true; bash golden has `complete -F`; zsh golden has `compdef`
- [PASS] ValidArgsFunction — both `goCmd` and `devCmd` set `ValidArgsFunction: completeProjectNames` which loads config at TAB-time and returns `ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp`
- [PASS] TUI uses /dev/tty — `shell.OpenTTY()` opens `/dev/tty` read/write, sets lipgloss color profile from tty output
- [PASS] Cancel on Escape exits silently — `go.go:122` and `dev.go:99` return `nil` when `selectionResult.Cancelled`; `remove.go:96` same
- [PASS] Empty projects shows actionable message — all three commands (go/dev/remove) check `len(cfg.Projects) == 0` and return `fmt.Errorf("no projects configured\n\nAdd your first project:\n gpa <name> <path>...")`
- [PASS] Golden files exist and match — `testdata/golden_bash.txt` and `testdata/golden_zsh.txt` present with function + completion wiring blocks; golden tests pass

## WARNING
- [PASS] All packages have test files — 14 test files across all 9 non-main packages
- [PASS] TUI models tested with teatest — projectlist (5 tests: select, esc, ctrl+c, prefilter, empty) and setup (5 tests: save, esc, ctrl+c, prechecked, navigate)
- [WARN] go.mod specifies `go 1.25.2` — this is an unreleased Go version. The spec called for Go 1.22. This works on the local machine but may fail on CI runners that don't have this toolchain.
- [WARN] `setup` cancel returns `fmt.Errorf("setup cancelled")` — this is treated as an error (non-zero exit). Deviation #3 says cancel should exit silently (exit 0). The `gp`/`gpd`/`gpr` commands correctly return nil on cancel, but `setup` does not. This is a minor inconsistency.
- [WARN] Compiled binary `arielsurco-cli` exists in repo root — gitignored but present on disk (7.4MB). Not a code issue.
- [WARN] `gpr` has `ValidArgsFunction: completeProjectNames` — this was added post-spec (deviation #2) which is fine, but the spec delta table shows `gpr` HasCompletions=false, meaning shell-init does NOT emit completion wiring for `gpr`. The Cobra-level completion only works when calling `arielsurco-cli project remove <TAB>` directly, not via the `gpr` alias. This is consistent with the design but worth noting.

## SUGGESTIONS
- The `cmd/root_test.go` only checks that `Version` defaults to "dev" and that `Execute` is callable — it does not actually invoke `Execute`. Consider adding a test that runs `rootCmd` with `--version` flag to verify version output.
- The `cmd/project/dev.go` TTY path does not have the same "exact match then skip TUI" optimization that `go.go` has. In TTY mode with an exact project name arg, `gpd` still launches the TUI. This is not a bug per se (the TUI will show the matching project), but it is an inconsistency with `gp` behavior. Consider adding the same shortcut.
- No LICENSE file exists in the repo, but `.goreleaser.yaml` references `LICENSE` in archives.files. The release build may warn or fail if LICENSE is missing.
- Consider adding a `.arielsurco-cli` compiled binary to `.gitignore` more robustly (e.g. `/arielsurco-cli` with leading slash to be explicit about root-only).

## Confirmed Deviations (not bugs)
1. `gp` TTY + exact arg: cd directly without TUI — implemented in go.go:77-87
2. `gpr` mirrors `gp` smart behavior (TTY exact match, fuzzy, TUI) — implemented in remove.go:57-75
3. Cancel (Escape) returns nil (exit 0) — implemented in go.go:122, dev.go:99, remove.go:96 (NOTE: setup.go returns error on cancel, see WARNING)
4. `gpa` resolves relative paths to absolute — `filepath.Abs(path)` in service.go:25
5. Homebrew tap uses same repo `ArielSurco/cli` — goreleaser.yaml repository.name = "cli"
6. `IsInteractiveSession()` (stdin) used for TUI decision — go.go:37, dev.go:36, remove.go:36
7. `/dev/tty` + lipgloss color profile from tty — shell.OpenTTY() in detect.go:40-49

## Test Results
```
ok  github.com/ArielSurco/cli/cmd                       (14 tests)
ok  github.com/ArielSurco/cli/cmd/project                (19 tests)
ok  github.com/ArielSurco/cli/internal/config             (7 tests)
ok  github.com/ArielSurco/cli/internal/module             (8 tests)
ok  github.com/ArielSurco/cli/internal/project           (11 tests)
ok  github.com/ArielSurco/cli/internal/shell             (10 tests)
ok  github.com/ArielSurco/cli/internal/tui/projectlist    (5 tests)
ok  github.com/ArielSurco/cli/internal/tui/setup          (5 tests)
All 79 tests pass. 0 failures. Linter: 0 issues. GoReleaser: valid.
```

## Summary
The implementation is solid and passes all critical checks. Every spec requirement from the design and task list is addressed in code with matching tests. The two warnings worth attention are: (1) `go 1.25.2` in go.mod may cause CI issues on standard runners, and (2) `setup` cancel returns an error instead of nil, inconsistent with the other commands' cancel behavior. A missing LICENSE file could cause goreleaser archive packaging issues at release time.

observations.title: sdd/go-cli/archive
observations.content:
# Archive: arielsurco-cli v0.1.0

**Change**: go-cli initial implementation
**Status**: COMPLETE
**Archived**: 2026-04-02

## What was built

A production-ready Go CLI tool (`arielsurco-cli`) for managing development projects with smart shell integration, TUI navigation, and tab completions. The binary provides four aliases (`gp`, `gpd`, `gpa`, `gpr`) for project navigation, development command execution, and project management. Config is stored in TOML with project-local overrides, and all shell wrappers are generated at runtime from a compiled-in registry to ensure binary-version coherence.

## Key architectural decisions

- **Compiled-in registry**: All shell wrappers (functions, completions, aliases) generated at runtime from `internal/module/Registry[]` — eliminates file management, ensures binary/config sync
- **Atomic TOML persistence**: Write to temp file then `os.Rename()` — prevents partial writes on crash
- **XDG paths via adrg/xdg**: No hardcoded `~/.config`; paths reload before each access for test isolation
- **CommandDef struct**: Each command has `NeedsEval` (gpd only) and `HasCompletions` bools — drives Generate() output for bash/zsh
- **TTY detection + `/dev/tty`**: IsTerminal() on stdout; TUI opens `/dev/tty` directly for color profile independence
- **Tab completions via Cobra `__complete`**: Called at TAB-time (zero caching); ValidArgsFunction loads config, returns project names + `ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp`
- **TUI models immutable**: Data injected at construction, result read from FinalModel() — testable with teatest
- **Three command modes**: TTY exact match (direct), TTY no args (fuzzy TUI), non-TTY lookup (stdout for scripting)
- **Shell wrappers**: `gp`/`gpr` use cd-into output; `gpd` uses eval; `gpa`/`gpr` direct call; all have completion support where applicable

## Deviations from spec (confirmed, not bugs)

1. **Go version 1.25.2 in go.mod** — Spec requested 1.22; local toolchain has 1.25.2. This works on the developer's machine but may cause CI runner issues with older toolchains.
2. **`setup` command cancel returns error** — Other commands (gp/gpd/gpr) return nil on Escape (exit 0); `setup` returns `fmt.Errorf("setup cancelled")` (exit 1). Inconsistency noted but not a blocker for v0.1.0.
3. **`gpr` HasCompletions=false in wiring** — Design spec shows `gpr` with `HasCompletions=false`, so shell-init does NOT emit completion wiring for the `gpr` alias. Direct `arielsurco-cli project remove <TAB>` still works via Cobra's ValidArgsFunction, but the `gpr` alias itself has no tab completion. This is by design.
4. **No LICENSE file in repo** — .goreleaser.yaml references LICENSE in archives.files; this may warn or fail at release time.

## Final state

- **Tests**: 79 tests across 9 packages (14 test files), all passing
  - cmd: 14 tests
  - cmd/project: 19 tests
  - internal/config: 7 tests
  - internal/module: 8 tests
  - internal/project: 11 tests
  - internal/shell: 10 tests (golden file tests for bash/zsh generation)
  - internal/tui/projectlist: 5 tests (teatest)
  - internal/tui/setup: 5 tests (teatest)
- **Linter**: 0 issues (`golangci-lint run` clean)
- **GoReleaser**: valid (checked `goreleaser check`)
- **Version embedding**: ldflags configured; `arielsurco-cli --version` will show v0.1.0 on release builds
- **Binary builds**: 4 targets in .goreleaser.yaml (darwin+linux × amd64+arm64)

## Release

- **Tag**: v0.1.0 (pending push)
- **Install**: 
  ```bash
  brew tap ArielSurco/cli https://github.com/ArielSurco/cli
  brew install arielsurco-cli
  ```
  Then run `arielsurco-cli setup` to configure active modules.

- **Manual tap setup required**:
  1. Create GitHub repo `ArielSurco/homebrew-cli`
  2. Add `TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN` secret (PAT with repo write scope)
  3. Tag v0.1.0 to trigger first release

## Remaining backlog (post v0.1.0)

- **Alias customization TUI**: Allow user to toggle active modules (dev scripts) in `setup` command; persist to `~/.config/arielsurco-cli/active.toml [aliases]` section
- **`gpd` exact-match shortcut**: Mirror `gp` behavior (TTY + exact project arg → skip TUI, run script directly)
- **CI improvements**: Fix go.mod to Go 1.22 (or document 1.25.2 requirement); add LICENSE file; consider `.gitignore` for binary
- **Setup cancel consistency**: Make `setup` Escape return nil (exit 0) to match other commands
- **Test coverage expansion**: Add integration test for `arielsurco-cli --version` output; add golangci-lint run in CI

## Artifacts retained

- Proposal: architecture, stack, module system, shell wrappers design
- Spec (Delta): shell integration with tab completions, CommandDef structure, acceptance criteria
- Technical Design: package interfaces, dependency DAG, CommandDef table, Generate() logic, completion wiring, TTY decision tree, testing strategy
- Task Checklist: 6 phases, 48 tasks (all completed)
- Verify Report: test results (79 pass), linter (0 issues), GoReleaser validation, confirmed deviations
- Apply Progress: all phases complete, commit 82d74a9, tap setup notes

observations.content:
## Project: arielsurco-cli (go-cli)

**Re-initialized**: 2026-04-02

## Stack
- Language: Go 1.25.2
- Module: github.com/ArielSurco/cli
- CLI Framework: Cobra v1.10.2 + Fang v2 (charm.land/fang/v2)
- TUI: Bubbletea v1.3.10 + bubbles v1.0.0
- Styling: Lip Gloss v1.1.0 + charm.land/lipgloss/v2
- Config: go-toml/v2 v2.3.0 + XDG (github.com/adrg/xdg)
- Fuzzy search: sahilm/fuzzy
- Test helpers: charmbracelet/x/exp/teatest

## Architecture
- cmd/ — Cobra command wiring only, no business logic
- cmd/project/ — project subcommands: add, remove, go, dev
- internal/config/ — XDG + TOML atomic load/save
- internal/project/ — Service layer (Add/Remove/FindByName/List/DevCommand)
- internal/tui/projectlist/ — Bubbletea fuzzy project list
- internal/tui/setup/ — Setup TUI
- internal/shell/ — TTY detection + shell script generation
- internal/module/ — module registry (compiled-in modules)

## Current CLI Surface
| Alias | Cobra | Description |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| gp [name] | project go | Navigate to a project (TUI or direct) |
| gpd [name] | project dev | Run the project dev script (eval) |
| gpa <name> <path> | project add | Register a project |
| gpr [name] | project remove | Unregister a project |

## Conventions
- TDD: failing test before implementation. No exceptions.
- Linter: golangci-lint (standard + revive, misspell, nolintlint, gocritic), 0 issues required
- Atomic commits: conventional commits, one commit per feature/fix
- Naming: no single-letter variables
- TTY detection: TUI mode when isTerminal=true, clean output when false
- Shell wrappers: functions for most commands, eval only for dev (needs env var propagation)
- Tests calling config.Save() MUST have t.Setenv("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", t.TempDir())

## Project Skills
| Skill | When |
|-------|------|
| go-testing | Writing or modifying any test |
| shell-integration | Shell wrappers, TTY detection, shell-init, eval rules |
| tui-patterns | Creating or modifying Bubbletea models |
| module-extension | Adding a new command or module |
| config-patterns | Config, XDG, atomic save, Service layer |
| release | Preparing a release or modifying CI |
